title: Maze Product Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Transfer Build
summary: An independent accessibility club receives a different grid, seed, algorithm, input profile, display scale, assistance need, platform, and failure condition and transfers maze product outcomes, repository baseline, accessibility, and evidence into a changed player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress thro…
objectives: Define player, task, puzzle rules, success, failure, session length, and observable value before selecting algorithms.
Inspect revision, status, environment, dependencies, licenses, existing tests, and recovery anchors before changing the project.
Record keyboard, pointer, vision, color, motion, timing, cognition, text, and assistive-technology needs as design inputs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Player Outcome
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab puzzle team handles case 4511 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Scope Transfer
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-scope-transfer-1lgwa8" that uses case 9583 to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Scope Transfer.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Scope Transfer into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Scope Transfer, keep Scope Transfer connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-scope-transfer-1lgwa8
# Competency: maze-scope-transfer.
# Case 9583: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_scope_transfer_1lgwa8(player_task: str = "compare-solvers", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, native_limits_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1lgwa8 = "1lgwa8-8awgl1-9583"
    assert evidence_variant_1lgwa8
    evidence_axes_1lgwa8 = (
        "grid1lgwa8",
        "topology9583",
        "search8awgl1",
        "event1lgwa89583",
        "access95831lgwa8",
        "recovery8awgl19583",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1lgwa8)) == 6
    if not player_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not native_limits_named:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "native-overclaim"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-maze-charter"
